title: bauhinia forficata subsp. pruinosa
intro: Bauhinia forficata subsp. pruinosa, also known as "pata de vaca" or cow's foot in English, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antihelmintic properties. The health benefits of Bauhinia forficata subsp. pruinosa include treating various health issues such as fever, diarrhea, and intestinal parasites, as well as promoting wound healing and reducing pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be made into tinctures and ointments for topical application.
